content.title: Sheet glass manufacture: the float process

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Glass, which has been made since the time of the Mesopotamians and Egyptians, is little more than a mixture of sand, soda ash and lime. When heated to about 1500 degrees Celsius (℃) this becomes a molten mass that hardens when slowly cooled. The first successful method for making clear, flat glass involved spinning. This method was very effective as the glass had not touched any surfaces between being soft and becoming hard, so it stayed perfectly unblemished, with a 'fire finish'. However, the process took a long time and was labour intensive.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Nevertheless, demand for flat glass was very high and glassmakers across the world were looking for a method of making it continuously. The first continuous ribbon process involved squeezing molten glass through two hot rollers, similar to an old mangle. This allowed glass of virtually any thickness to be made non-stop, but the rollers would leave both sides of the glass marked, and these would then need to be ground and polished. This part of the process rubbed away around 20 per cent of the glass, and the machines were very expensive.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The float process for making flat glass was invented by Alistair Pilkington. This process allows the manufacture of clear, tinted and coated glass for buildings, and clear and tinted glass for vehicles. Pilkington had been experimenting with improving the melting process, and in 1952 he had the idea of using a bed of molten metal to form the flat glass, eliminating altogether the need for rollers within the float bath. The metal had to melt at a temperature less than the hardening point of glass (about 600°C), but could not boil at a temperature below the temperature of the molten glass (about 1500°C). The best metal for the job was tin.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: The rest of the concept relied on gravity, which guaranteed that the surface of the molten metal was perfectly flat and horizontal. Consequently, when pouring molten glass onto the molten tin, the underside of the glass would also be perfectly flat. If the glass were kept hot enough, it would flow over the molten tin until the top surface was also flat, horizontal and perfectly parallel to the bottom surface. Once the glass cooled to 604°C or less it was too hard to mark and could be transported out of the cooling zone by rollers. The glass settled to a thickness of six millimetres because of surface tension interactions between the glass and the tin. By fortunate coincidence, 60 per cent of the flat glass market at that time was for six-millimetre glass.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Pilkington built a pilot plant in 1953 and by 1955 he had convinced his company to build a full-scale plant. However, it took 14 months of non-stop production, costing the company £100,000 a month, before the plant produced any usable glass. Furthermore, once they succeeded in making marketable flat glass, the machine was turned off for a service to prepare it for years of continuous production. When it started up again it took another four months to get the process right again. They finally succeeded in 1959 and there are now float plants all over the world, with each able to produce around 1000 tons of glass every day, non-stop for around 15 years.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Float plants today make glass of near optical quality. Several processes - melting, refining, homogenising - take place simultaneously in the 2000 tonnes of molten glass in the furnace. They occur in separate zones in a complex glass flow driven by high temperatures. It adds up to a continuous melting process, lasting as long as 50 hours, that delivers glass smoothly and continuously to the float bath, and from there to a coating zone and finally a heat treatment zone, where stresses formed during cooling are relieved.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The principle of float glass is unchanged since the 1950s. However, the product has changed dramatically, from a single thickness of 6.8 mm to a range from sub-millimetre to 25 mm, from a ribbon frequently marred by inclusions and bubbles to almost optical perfection. To ensure the highest quality, inspection takes place at every stage. Occasionally, a bubble is not removed during refining, a sand grain refuses to melt, a tremor in the tin puts ripples into the glass ribbon. Automated on-line inspection does two things. Firstly, it reveals process faults upstream that can be corrected. Inspection technology allows more than 100 million measurements a second to be made across the ribbon, locating flaws the unaided eye would be unable to see. Secondly, it enables computers downstream to steer cutters around flaws.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Float glass is sold by the square metre, and at the final stage computers translate customer requirements into patterns of cuts designed to minimise waste.

groups.title: Early methods of producing flat glass
groups.desc:

questions.analysis: 题目要求填空的部分是第1题，图中显示的是玻璃制造的一个步骤。根据文章内容，最早成功制造透明平板玻璃的方法是“spinning”。我们可以在文章中找到以下句子：“The first successful method for making clear, flat glass involved spinning.” 题干中的“first successful method for making clear, flat glass”与文章中的“first successful method for making clear, flat glass”完全对应，因此答案是“spinning”。

central_sentences.sentence: The first successful method for making clear, flat glass involved spinning.
central_sentences.translation: 第一个成功制造透明平板玻璃的方法是旋转法。

target_sen.target_sen: The first successful method for making clear, flat glass involved spinning.
The first successful method for making clear, flat glass involved spinning.

questions.analysis: 在题干中，6的位置是在玻璃的表面，且在melting zone（熔化区）之后，cooling zone（冷却区）之前。我们需要找到描述玻璃表面状态的词语。文章中提到“the glass had not touched any surfaces between being soft and becoming hard, so it stayed perfectly unblemished”，这里的“perfectly unblemished”描述了玻璃表面没有任何瑕疵，符合题干中6的位置所需要的描述。因此，正确答案是“unblemished”或“perfectly unblemished”。

central_sentences.sentence: This method was very effective as the glass had not touched any surfaces between being soft and becoming hard, so it stayed perfectly unblemished, with a 'fire finish'.
central_sentences.translation: 这种方法非常有效，因为玻璃在从软变硬的过程中没有接触任何表面，所以它保持了完美无瑕的状态，具有“火焰抛光”的效果。

target_sen.target_sen: This method was very effective as the glass had not touched any surfaces between being soft and becoming hard, so it stayed perfectly unblemished, with a 'fire finish'.
This method was very effective as the glass had not touched any surfaces between being soft and becoming hard, so it stayed perfectly unblemished, with a 'fire finish'.

questions.answer: labour-intensive / labor-intensive / labor intensive / labour intensive
questions.analysis: 题目中提到“the first successful method for making clear, flat glass”，在原文中可以找到完全相同的表达“The first successful method for making clear, flat glass involved spinning”。接下来，题目中提到“the process took a long time and was ________”，在原文中对应的句子是“However, the process took a long time and was labour intensive”。因此，答案是“labour intensive”。学生需要注意的是，虽然“took a long time”也可以理解为“耗时长”，但题目要求填入的词语需要准确描述“需要大量劳动力”的特点，而不仅仅是“耗时”。

central_sentences.sentence: The first successful method for making clear, flat glass involved spinning. This method was very effective as the glass had not touched any surfaces between being soft and becoming hard, so it stayed perfectly unblemished, with a 'fire finish'. However, the process took a long time and was labour intensive.
central_sentences.translation: 制造透明平板玻璃的第一个成功方法是旋转。这种方法非常有效，因为玻璃在从软变硬的过程中没有接触任何表面，因此保持了完美无瑕的“火焰表面”。然而，这个过程耗时很长，而且需要大量劳动力。

syn_ary.synonym: The first successful method for making clear, flat glass

target_sen.target_sen: The first successful method for making clear, flat glass involved spinning.

locating_words.locating_word: the first successful method for making clear, flat glass

target_sen.target_sen: However, the process took a long time and was labour intensive.

questions.analysis: 图示中箭头指向玻璃的厚度，题目要求填空的内容是描述玻璃的某个属性。在文章中，明确提到“the glass settled to a thickness of six millimetres”，这里的“thickness”直接对应了图示中箭头指向的内容。因此，正确答案是“thickness”。

central_sentences.sentence: The glass settled to a thickness of six millimetres because of surface tension interactions between the glass and the tin.
central_sentences.translation: 由于玻璃和锡之间的表面张力相互作用，玻璃稳定在六毫米的厚度。

target_sen.target_sen: Once the glass cooled to 604~C or less it was too hard to mark and could be transported out of the cooling zone by rollers~, The glass settled to a thickness of six millimetres because of surface tension interactions between the glass and the tin.

questions.analysis: 在题目中，冷却区（cooling zone）之后的玻璃通过两个滚轮（rollers）。文章中提到滚轮会在玻璃的两面留下痕迹（marked）。因此，正确答案是“marked”。

central_sentences.sentence: The first continuous ribbon process involved squeezing molten glass through two hot rollers, similar to an old mangle. This allowed glass of virtually any thickness to be made non-stop, but the rollers would leave both sides of the glass marked, and these would then need to be ground and polished.
central_sentences.translation: 第一个连续带状玻璃制造过程是将熔融玻璃通过两个热滚轮挤压，类似于旧式的轧布机。这使得几乎任何厚度的玻璃都可以连续制造，但滚轮会在玻璃的两面留下痕迹，这些痕迹随后需要被打磨和抛光。

target_sen.target_sen: The first continuous ribbon process involved squeezing molten glass through two hot rollers, similar to an old mangle.
This allowed glass of virtually any thickness to be made non-stop, but the rollers would leave both sides of the glass marked, and these would then need to be ground and polished.

questions.analysis: 图示中的第6题位于“melting zone”之前，表示进入熔化区的物质。文章中提到“when pouring molten glass onto the molten tin”，这里的“pouring molten glass”与图示中的位置和过程相符。因此，第6题的答案是“glass”或“molten glass”。学生需要注意的是，虽然文章中提到“tin”和“metal”，但这些是玻璃的承载物，而不是进入熔化区的物质。

central_sentences.sentence: Consequently, when pouring molten glass onto the molten tin, the underside of the glass would also be perfectly flat.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，当将熔融玻璃倒入熔融锡上时，玻璃的底面也会变得非常平整。

target_sen.target_sen: Consequently, when pouring molten glass onto the molten tin, the underside of the glass would also be perfectly flat.

questions.answer: tin / molten tin / metal / molten metal
questions.analysis: 在图中，7的位置位于玻璃的下方，处于“melting zone”（熔化区）和“cooling zone”（冷却区）之间。根据文章内容，描述玻璃在熔化区和冷却区之间的材料是“molten tin”（熔化的锡）。文章中提到“when pouring molten glass onto the molten tin, the underside of the glass would also be perfectly flat”，这说明在玻璃的下方是熔化的锡，保证了玻璃的平整。因此，正确答案是“tin”或“molten tin”。

central_sentences.sentence: The rest of the concept relied on gravity, which guaranteed that the surface of the molten metal was perfectly flat and horizontal. Consequently, when pouring molten glass onto the molten tin, the underside of the glass would also be perfectly flat.
central_sentences.translation: 其余的概念依赖于重力，这保证了熔化金属的表面是完全平坦和水平的。因此，当将熔化的玻璃倒在熔化的锡上时，玻璃的底面也会完全平坦。

target_sen.target_sen: Once the glass cooled to 604~C or less it was too hard to mark and could be transported out of the cooling zone by rollers~, The glass settled to a thickness of six millimetres because of surface tension interactions between the glass and the tin.

questions.analysis: 图示显示玻璃在冷却区之后通过两个圆柱形物体。根据文章内容，最直接对应的句子是“Once the glass cooled to 604~C or less it was too hard to mark and could be transported out of the cooling zone by rollers.” 这句话中的“transported out of the cooling zone by rollers”与图示中的冷却区和两个圆柱形物体直接对应。因此，答案是“rollers”。

central_sentences.sentence: Once the glass cooled to 604~C or less it was too hard to mark and could be transported out of the cooling zone by rollers~, The
central_sentences.translation: 一旦玻璃冷却到604摄氏度或更低，它就变得太硬而无法被标记，并且可以通过滚筒从冷却区运输出去。

target_sen.target_sen: Once the glass cooled to 604~C or less it was too hard to mark and could be transported out of the cooling zone by rollers~, The glass settled to a thickness of six millimetres because of surface tension interactions between the glass and the tin.

Once the glass cooled to 604~C or less it was too hard to mark and could be transported out of the cooling zone by rollers~, The glass settled to a thickness of six millimetres because of surface tension interactions between the glass and the tin.

groups.desc:
Questions 9-13

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
Choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information given in the text,
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information,
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.

questions.title: The metal used in the float process had to have specific properties.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息 "The metal used in the float process had to have specific properties" 在文章中有明确对应。文章描述了使用的金属需要满足特定的温度属性：熔点和沸点。这与题干中的 "specific properties" 对应。因此，答案是 TRUE。学生需要通过识别关键字 "metal" 和 "specific properties" 以及理解段落大意来找到对应信息，从而做出正确判断。

central_sentences.sentence: The metal had to melt at a temperature less than the hardening point of glass (about 600~C), but could not boil at a temperature below the temperature of the molten glass (about 1500~C).
central_sentences.translation: 这种金属必须在低于玻璃硬化点（约600℃）的温度下熔化，但不能在低于熔融玻璃温度（约1500℃）的温度下沸腾。

target_sen.target_sen: Pilkington had been experimenting with improving the melting process, and in 1952 he had the idea of using a bed of molten metal to form the flat glass, eliminating altogether the need for rollers within the float bath.
The metal had to melt at a temperature less than the hardening point of glass (about 600~C), but could not boil at a temperature below the temperature of the molten glass (about 1500~C).
The best metal for the job was tin.

syn_ary.synonym: melt at a temperature less than

target_sen.target_sen: The metal had to melt at a temperature less than the hardening point of glass (about 600~C), but could not boil at a temperature below the temperature of the molten glass (about 1500~C).

syn_ary.synonym: could not boil at a temperature below

target_sen.target_sen: The metal had to melt at a temperature less than the hardening point of glass (about 600~C), but could not boil at a temperature below the temperature of the molten glass (about 1500~C).

questions.title: Pilkington invested some of his own money in his float plant.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提到Pilkington是否使用了自己的钱来投资浮法玻璃厂。虽然文章提到他在1953年建了一个试验工厂，并在1955年说服公司建了一个全规模的工厂，但没有提到他个人的资金投入。因此，无法确定题干中的信息是否正确。

central_sentences.sentence: Pilkington built a pilot plant in 1953 and by 1955 he had convinced his company to build a full-scale plant.
central_sentences.translation: Pilkington在1953年建造了一个试验工厂，到1955年，他说服了他的公司建造一个全规模的工厂。

target_sen.target_sen: Pilkington built a pilot plant in 1953 and by 1955 he had convinced his company to build a full-scale plant.
Pilkington built a pilot plant in 1953 and by 1955 he had convinced his company to build a full-scale plant.
Pilkington built a pilot plant in 1953 and by 1955 he had convinced his company to build a full-scale plant.

questions.title: Pilkington's first full-scale plant was an instant commercial success.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“instant commercial success”表示“即刻商业成功”，而原文中提到工厂花了14个月的持续生产和高昂的成本才生产出可用的玻璃，这与“即刻商业成功”相矛盾。因此，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: However, it took 14 months of non-stop production, costing the company ￡100, 000 a month, before the plant produced any usable glass. Furthermore
central_sentences.translation: 然而，这个工厂在生产出任何可用的玻璃之前，经历了14个月的不间断生产，每月耗费公司10万英镑。

central_sentences.sentence: Furthermore, once they succeeded in making marketable flat glass, the machine was turned off for a service to prepare it for years of continuous production. When it started up again it took another four months to get the process right again.
central_sentences.translation: 此外，一旦他们成功生产出可销售的平板玻璃，机器就被关闭进行维护，以准备多年的连续生产。当它再次启动时，又花了四个月才重新调整好生产流程。

target_sen.target_sen: Pilkington built a pilot plant in 1953 and by 1955 he had convinced his company to build a full-scale plant.
Pilkington built a pilot plant in 1953 and by 1955 he had convinced his company to build a full-scale plant.
However, it took 14 months of non-stop production, costing the company ￡100, 000 a month, before the plant produced any usable glass.
However, it took 14 months of non-stop production, costing the company ￡100, 000 a month, before the plant produced any usable glass.

questions.title: The process invented by Pilkington has now been improved.
questions.analysis: 答案是 TRUE，因为文章明确提到虽然浮法玻璃的基本原理没有改变，但产品质量和生产过程已经有了显著的改进和提高。题干中的 "process" 对应原文中的 "principle"，"improved" 对应原文中的 "changed dramatically"、"near optical quality" 和 "automated on-line inspection"。这些同义替换词表明了浮法玻璃生产过程的改进。

central_sentences.sentence: The principle of float glass is unchanged since the 1950s. However, the product has changed dramatically, from a single thickness of 6. 8 mm to a range from sub-millimetre to 25 mm, from a ribbon frequently marred by inclusions and bubbles to almost optical perfection.
central_sentences.translation: 自20世纪50年代以来，浮法玻璃的原理没有改变。然而，产品已经发生了巨大的变化，从单一的6.8毫米厚度变为从亚毫米到25毫米的范围，从经常被夹杂物和气泡损坏的玻璃带变为几乎达到光学完美的程度。

target_sen.target_sen: The principle of float glass is unchanged since the 1950s.
However, the product has changed dramatically, from a single thickness of 6.

questions.title: Computers are better than humans at detecting faults in glass.
questions.analysis: 答案是 TRUE，因为文章明确提到 "Automated on-line inspection"（自动在线检查）能够发现 "the unaided eye would be unable to see"（肉眼无法看到）的缺陷。这表明计算机在检测玻璃缺陷方面优于人类。这直接对应了题干中的信息 "Computers are better than humans at detecting faults in glass."。

central_sentences.sentence: Inspection technology allows more than 100 million measurements a second to be made across the ribbon, locating flaws the unaided eye would be unable to see.
central_sentences.translation: 检测技术每秒可以在玻璃带上进行超过1亿次测量，能够发现肉眼无法看到的缺陷。

target_sen.target_sen: Automated on-line inspection does two things.
Inspection technology allows more than 100 million measurements a second to be made across the ribbon, locating flaws the unaided eye would be unable to see.
Inspection technology allows more than 100 million measurements a second to be made across the ribbon, locating flaws the unaided eye would be unable to see.
Firstly, it reveals process faults upstream that can be corrected.
Inspection technology allows more than 100 million measurements a second to be made across the ribbon, locating flaws the unaided eye would be unable to see.

content.title: THE LITTLE ICE AGE
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了人类在整个存在过程中一直受到气候变化的影响，尽管我们通常认为气候是不变的，但在过去73万年中至少发生了八次冰期。我们的祖先通过适应全球变暖的机会，发展了生存策略，如耐旱、耐寒和农业养殖，但突然的气候变化也带来了饥荒、疾病和苦难的代价。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了小冰河期的持续时间、其对欧洲的影响以及它对当前全球变暖的重要性，同时指出小冰河期并非持续的严寒，而是由大气和海洋之间复杂而尚不完全理解的相互作用驱动的不规则的快速气候变化。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了过去气候变化的重建非常困难，因为系统性的天气观测仅在几个世纪前在欧洲和北美开始，印度和热带非洲的记录甚至更近。在记录开始之前的时间里，我们只有主要通过树木年轮和冰芯重建的“代理记录”，再加上一些不完整的书面记载。现在我们已经有了来自整个北半球的数百个树木年轮记录，以及来自赤道以南地区的许多记录，还有来自南极洲、格陵兰、秘鲁安第斯山脉和其他地点的冰芯温度数据的不断增加。我们已经接近了对北半球大部分地区600年来夏季和冬季温度变化的了解。', 'D': '这本书主要讲述了过去十个世纪气候变化的叙述性历史，以及欧洲人适应这些变化的一些方式。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了小冰期对欧洲的影响，包括北欧航行受阻、北大西洋和北海的风暴增加、欧洲陷入饥荒、渔业受到影响以及北欧农业革命的兴起等内容。\n\n总结：小冰期对欧洲的气候和经济产生了重大影响，导致航行受阻、饥荒蔓延、渔业和农业发生变革。', 'F': '全球气温在1850年后开始缓慢上升，随着现代暖期的开始。大量来自欧洲的渴望土地的农民和其他人移民到北美、澳大利亚、新西兰和南非，其中爱尔兰马铃薯疫病引起的饥荒也起到了推动作用。1850年至1890年间，随着欧洲密集农业方法的扩展，数百万公顷的森林和林地被新来者的斧头砍伐。前所未有的土地开垦释放了大量二氧化碳进入大气中，首次引发了人为引起的全球变暖。随着化石燃料的使用普及和温室气体水平继续飙升，20世纪的气温上升更加迅速。自20世纪80年代初以来，上升速度更加陡峭。小冰河时代已经让位给一个新的气候时期，以持续而稳定的升温为特征。与此同时，5级飓风等极端天气事件变得更加频繁。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: This book will provide a detailed examination of the Little Ice Age and other climatic shifts, but, before I embark on that, let me provide a historical context. We tend to think of climate - as opposed to weather - as something unchanging, yet humanity has been at the mercy of climate change for its entire existence, with at least eight glacial episodes in the past 730,000 years. Our ancestors adapted to the universal but irregular global warming since the end of the last great Ice Age, around 10,000 years ago, with dazzling opportunism. They developed strategies for surviving harsh drought cycles, decades of heavy rainfall or unaccustomed cold; adopted agriculture and stock-raising, which revolutionised human life; and founded the world's first pre-industrial civilisations in Egypt, Mesopotamia and the Americas. But the price of sudden climate change, in famine, disease and suffering, was often high.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The Little Ice Age lasted from roughly 1300 until the middle of the nineteenth century. Only two centuries ago, Europe experienced a cycle of bitterly cold winters; mountain glaciers in the Swiss Alps were the lowest in recorded memory, and pack ice surrounded Iceland for much of the year. The climatic events of the Little Ice Age did more than help shape the modern world. They are the deeply important context for the current unprecedented global warming. The Little Ice Age was far from a deep freeze, however; rather an irregular seesaw of rapid climatic shifts, few lasting more than a quarter-century, driven by complex and still little understood interactions between the atmosphere and the ocean. The seesaw brought cycles of intensely cold winters and easterly winds, then switched abruptly to years of heavy spring and early summer rains, mild winters, and frequent Atlantic storms, or to periods of droughts, light northeasterly winds, and summer heat waves.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Reconstructing the climate changes of the past is extremely difficult, because systematic weather observations began only a few centuries ago, in Europe and North America. Records from India and tropical Africa are even more recent. For the time before records began, we have only 'proxy records' reconstructed largely from tree rings and ice cores, supplemented by a few incomplete written accounts. We now have hundreds of tree-ring records from throughout the northern hemisphere, and many from south of the equator, too, amplified with a growing body of temperature data from ice cores drilled in Antarctica, Greenland, the Peruvian Andes, and other locations. We are close to a knowledge of annual summer and winter temperature variations over much of the northern hemisphere going back 600 years.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: This book is a narrative history of climatic shifts during the past ten centuries, and some of the ways in which people in Europe adapted to them. Part One describes the Medieval Warm Period, roughly 900 to 1200. During these three centuries, Norse voyagers from Northern Europe explored northern seas, settled Greenland, and visited North America. It was not a time of uniform warmth, for then, as always since the Great Ice Age, there were constant shifts in rainfall and temperature. Mean European temperatures were about the same as today, perhaps slightly cooler.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: It is known that the Little Ice Age cooling began in Greenland and the Arctic in about 1200. As the Arctic ice pack spread southward, Norse voyages to the west were rerouted into the open Atlantic, then ended altogether. Storminess increased in the North Atlantic and North Sea. Colder, much wetter weather descended on Europe between 1315 and 1319, when thousands perished in a continent-wide famine. By 1400, the weather had become decidedly more unpredictable and stormier, with sudden shifts and lower temperatures that culminated in the cold decades of the late sixteenth century. Fish were a vital commodity in growing towns and cities, where food supplies were a constant concern. Dried cod and herring were already the staples of the European fish trade, but changes in water temperatures forced fishing fleets to work further offshore. The Basques, Dutch, and English developed the first offshore fishing boats adapted to a colder and stormier Atlantic. A gradual agricultural revolution in northern Europe stemmed from concerns over food supplies at a time of rising populations. The revolution involved intensive commercial farming and the growing of animal fodder on land not previously used for crops. The increased productivity from farmland made some countries self-sufficient in grain and livestock and offered effective protection against famine.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Global temperatures began to rise slowly after 1850, with the beginning of the Modern Warm Period. There was a vast migration from Europe by land-hungry farmers and others, to which the famine caused by the Irish potato blight contributed, to North America, Australia, New Zealand, and southern Africa. Millions of hectares of forest and woodland fell before the newcomers' axes between 1850 and 1890, as intensive European farming methods expanded across the world. The unprecedented land clearance released vast quantities of carbon dioxide into the atmosphere, triggering for the first time humanly caused global warming. Temperatures climbed more rapidly in the twentieth century as the use of fossil fuels proliferated and greenhouse gas levels continued to soar. The rise has been even steeper since the early 1980s. The Little Ice Age has given way to a new climatic regime, marked by prolonged and steady warming. At the same time, extreme weather events like Category 5 hurricanes are becoming more frequent.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 14-17

The text has 6 sections. 
Choose the correct heading for each section and move it into the gap.

Example Answer
Paragraph A viii - People have always responded to climate change
Paragraph C v - How past climatic conditions can be determined

list.text: The relevance of the Little Ice Age today
How cities contribute to climate change
How past climatic conditions can be determined
A growing need for weather records
A study covering a thousand years
People have always responded to climate change

questions.analysis: Section B的核心在于讨论小冰期对当前全球变暖的重要性和相关性。文章明确提到“小冰期的气候事件不仅仅帮助塑造了现代世界。它们是当前前所未有的全球变暖的重要背景。”这与小标题ii: The relevance of the Little Ice Age today直接对应。通过识别段落中的关键词和理解中心思想，学生可以更容易地找到正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: The climatic events of the Little Ice Age did more than help shape the modern world. They are the deeply important context for the current unprecedented global warming.
central_sentences.translation: 小冰期的气候事件不仅帮助塑造了现代世界，它们还为当前前所未有的全球变暖提供了深远的重要背景。

target_sen.target_sen: They are the deeply important context for the current unprecedented global warming.
The Little Ice Age lasted from roughly 1300 until the middle of the nineteenth century.
The climatic events of the Little Ice Age did more than help shape the modern world.

The Little Ice Age was far from a deep freeze, however; rather an irregular seesaw of rapid climatic shifts, few lasting more than a quarter-century, driven by complex and still little understood interactions between the atmosphere and the ocean.
They are the deeply important context for the current unprecedented global warming.

questions.analysis: Section D的主要内容是对过去千年气候变化的研究，这与小标题vii: A study covering a thousand years直接对应。通过识别关键表达“ten centuries”和“a study covering a thousand years”的同义关系，学生可以确定正确答案。即使词汇量有限，学生也可以通过抓住关键词和排除不相关选项来答对这道题。

central_sentences.sentence: This book is a narrative history of climatic shifts during the past ten centuries, and some of the ways in which people in Europe adapted to them.
central_sentences.translation: 这本书是过去十个世纪气候变化的叙述历史，以及欧洲人如何适应这些变化的一些方式。

target_sen.target_sen: This book is a narrative history of climatic shifts during the past ten centuries, and some of the ways in which people in Europe adapted to them.

questions.analysis: Section E的核心内容是农业革命带来的粮食自给和防止饥荒的措施。文章提到“self-sufficient in grain and livestock”和“effective protection against famine”，这与小标题“Enough food at last”直接对应。通过识别这些关键同义词，可以确定Section E的最佳标题是“ix: Enough food at last”。

groups.collect: Documentation of past weather conditions is limited: our main sources of knowledge of conditions in the distant past are $1614$and $1615$. We can deduce that the Little Ice Age was a time of $1616$, rather than of consistent freezing. Within it there were some periods of very cold winters, others of $1617$and heavy rain, and yet others that saw $1618$with no rain at all.

central_sentences.sentence: The Little Ice Age was far from a deep freeze, however; rather an irregular seesaw of rapid climatic shifts, few lasting more than a quarter-century, driven by complex and still little understood interactions between the atmosphere and the ocean.

content.title: The meaning and power of smell
content.summary: {'A': '这段话主要讲述了气味对人们情感的影响，气味可以唤起强烈的情感反应，与好的经历相关的气味可以带来喜悦，而与不好的记忆相关的气味可能会引起厌恶。人们对气味的喜好和厌恶往往基于情感关联，这种关联可以强大到我们通常认为令人不快的气味变得令人愉悦，而我们通常认为芳香的气味对某些人来说却变得令人不悦。因此，对气味的感知不仅包括气味本身的感觉，还包括与之相关的经历和情感。', 'B': '气味也是社交联系中必不可少的线索，婴儿在出生后很快就能辨认出母亲的气味，成年人也常常能通过气味辨认出自己的孩子或配偶。在一个著名的测试中，女性和男性能够仅凭气味区分出自己配偶穿过的衣物和其他人穿过的类似衣物。大多数受试者在参与测试之前可能从未考虑过气味作为识别家庭成员的线索，但实验揭示出，即使在没有意识到的情况下，气味也会被记录下来。', 'C': '尽管嗅觉对我们的情感和感官生活非常重要，但在许多文化中，嗅觉可能是最被低估的感官。与动物相比，人类的嗅觉能力虽然不如某些动物那样精细，但仍然非常敏锐，我们的鼻子能够识别成千上万种气味，并感知到只存在极小量的气味。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了嗅觉是一种难以捉摸的现象，因为很多语言中并没有特定的词汇来描述气味，也无法记录气味，这对嗅觉研究有着重要影响。', 'E': '这段话主要讲述了迄今为止关于嗅觉的大部分研究都是以物理科学为主，虽然在对嗅觉的生物学和化学本质的理解方面取得了重要进展，但仍有许多基本问题有待解答，如嗅觉是一种还是两种感觉，嗅觉是否只影响鼻子等。这些问题意味着对嗅觉心理学的兴趣将不可避免地在研究中扮演越来越重要的角色。', 'F': '这段话主要讲述了嗅觉不仅仅是一种生物和心理现象，而是一种文化现象，因此具有社会和历史意义；不同文化对气味的价值观不同，我们对气味的感知是与世界互动的一种方式，也是人类文化本质的研究对象。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: A survey conducted by Anthony Synott at Montreal's Concordia University asked participants to comment on how important smell was to them in their lives. It became apparent that smell can evoke strong emotional responses. A scent associated with a good experience can bring a rush of joy, while a foul odour or one associated with a bad memory may make us grimace with disgust. Respondents to the survey noted that many of their olfactory likes and dislikes were based on emotional associations. Such associations can be powerful enough so that odours that we would generally label unpleasant become agreeable, and those that we would generally consider fragrant become disagreeable for particular individuals. The perception of smell, therefore, consists not only of the sensation of the odours themselves, but of the experiences and emotions associated with them.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Odours are also essential cues in social bonding. One respondent to the survey believed that there is no true emotional bonding without touching and smelling a loved one. In fact, infants recognise the odours of their mothers soon after birth and adults can often identify their children or spouses by scent. In one well-known test, women and men were able to distinguish by smell alone clothing worn by their marriage partners from similar clothing worn by other people. Most of the subjects would probably never have given much thought to odour as a cue for identifying family members before being involved in the test, but as the experiment revealed, even when not consciously considered, smells register.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: In spite of its importance to our emotional and sensory lives, smell is probably the most undervalued sense in many cultures. The reason often given for the low regard in which smell is held is that, in comparison with its importance among animals, the human sense of smell is feeble and undeveloped. While it is true that the olfactory powers of humans are nothing like as fine as those possessed by certain animals, they are still remarkably acute. Our noses are able to recognise thousands of smells, and to perceive odours which are present only in extremely small quantities.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Smell, however, is a highly elusive phenomenon. Odours, unlike colours, for instance, cannot be named in many languages because the specific vocabulary simply doesn't exist. 'It smells like...,' we have to say when describing an odour, struggling to express our olfactory experience. Nor can odours be recorded: there is no effective way to either capture or store them over time. In the realm of olfaction, we must make do with descriptions and recollections. This has implications for olfactory research.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Most of the research on smell undertaken to date has been of a physical scientific nature. Significant advances have been made in the understanding of the biological and chemical nature of olfaction, but many fundamental questions have yet to be answered. Researchers have still to decide whether smell is one sense or two—one responding to odours proper and the other registering odourless chemicals in the air. Other unanswered questions are whether the nose is the only part of the body affected by odours, and how smells can be measured objectively given the nonphysical components. Questions like these mean that interest in the psychology of smell is inevitably set to play an increasingly important role for researchers.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: However, smell is not simply a biological and psychological phenomenon. Smell is cultural, hence it is a social and historical phenomenon. Odours are invested with cultural values: smells that are considered to be offensive in some cultures may be perfectly acceptable in others. Therefore, our sense of smell is a means of, and model for, interacting with the world. Different smells can provide us with intimate and emotionally charged experiences and the value that we attach to these experiences is interiorised by the members of society in a deeply personal way. Importantly, our commonly held feelings about smells can help distinguish us from other cultures. The study of the cultural history of smell is, therefore, in a very real sense, an investigation into the essence of human culture.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 27-32

The text has 6 sections. 
Choose the correct heading for each section and move it into the gap.

list.text: The difficulties of talking about smells
The role of smell in personal relationships
The relationship between the brain and the nose
The interpretation of smells as a factor in defining groups
Why our sense of smell is not appreciated
The relationship between smell and feelings

questions.analysis: 第一部分的内容主要讨论了气味与情感之间的关系。文章明确提到气味可以引起强烈的情感反应，这与题干中的“气味与情感的关系”直接对应。因此，标题 viii: The relationship between smell and feelings 是最合适的。

central_sentences.sentence: It became apparent that smell can evoke strong emotional responses.
central_sentences.translation: 很明显，气味可以引发强烈的情感反应。

target_sen.target_sen: It became apparent that smell can evoke strong emotional responses.
A survey conducted by Anthony Synott at Montreal's Concordia University asked participants to comment on how important smell was to them in their lives.
It became apparent that smell can evoke strong emotional responses.
The perception of smell, therefore, consists not only of the sensation of the odours themselves, but of the experiences and emotions associated with them.
It became apparent that smell can evoke strong emotional responses.

questions.analysis: 这段文字主要讨论了气味在个人关系中的作用。通过提到“社会联系”（social bonding）和“情感联系”（emotional bonding），以及婴儿和成年人如何通过气味识别亲人，明确了气味在个人关系中的重要性。因此，标题 ii: The role of smell in personal relationships 是最合适的。

central_sentences.sentence: Odours are also essential cues in social bonding.
central_sentences.translation: 气味也是社交联系中的重要线索。

target_sen.target_sen: Odours are also essential cues in social bonding.
Odours are also essential cues in social bonding.
In fact, infants recognise the odours of their mothers soon after birth and adults can often identify their children or spouses by scent.
Odours are also essential cues in social bonding.

questions.analysis: Section C的主要内容是关于嗅觉为什么不被重视。文章中提到“smell is probably the most undervalued sense in many cultures”和“the reason often given for the low regard in which smell is held”这两句话，明确指出了嗅觉不被重视的原因。这与标题vi“Why our sense of smell is not appreciated”完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: In spite of its importance to our emotional and sensory lives, smell is probably the most undervalued sense in many cultures.
central_sentences.translation: 尽管嗅觉对我们的情感和感官生活很重要，但在许多文化中，它可能是最不被重视的感官。

target_sen.target_sen: In spite of its importance to our emotional and sensory lives, smell is probably the most undervalued sense in many cultures.
The reason often given for the low regard in which smell is held is that, in comparison with its importance among animals, the human sense of smell is feeble and undeveloped.

questions.analysis: 这段文字主要讨论了描述和记录气味的困难。关键词“difficulties”和“talking”在原文中分别对应“struggling”、“cannot be named”、“cannot be recorded”和“express”、“describing”。因此，这段文字的最佳标题是“i: The difficulties of talking about smells”。

central_sentences.sentence: Odours, unlike colours, for instance, cannot be named in many languages because the specific vocabulary simply doesn't exist. 'It smells like...,' we have to say when describing an odour, struggling to express our olfactory experience.
central_sentences.translation: 与颜色不同，在许多语言中气味是无法命名的，因为缺乏特定的词汇。当描述一种气味时，我们不得不说"闻起来像……"，努力表达我们的嗅觉体验。

target_sen.target_sen: Odours, unlike colours, for instance, cannot be named in many languages because the specific vocabulary simply doesn't exist.
'It smells like...,' we have to say when describing an odour, struggling to express our olfactory experience.
'It smells like...,' we have to say when describing an odour, struggling to express our olfactory experience.
'It smells like...,' we have to say when describing an odour, struggling to express our olfactory experience.
Nor can odours be recorded: there is no effective way to either capture or store them over time.
'It smells like...,' we have to say when describing an odour, struggling to express our olfactory experience.
This has implications for olfactory research.

questions.analysis: 这段文字主要讨论了嗅觉研究中的未解之谜和未来研究的方向。文中提到“许多基本问题尚未得到解答”和“对嗅觉心理学的兴趣不可避免地将对研究人员发挥越来越重要的作用”，这些都表明未来的研究方向。因此，小标题 iii "Future studies into smell" 是最合适的选择。

central_sentences.sentence: Questions like these mean that interest in the psychology of smell is inevitably set to play an increasingly important role for researchers.
central_sentences.translation: 这些问题意味着嗅觉心理学的研究必然会在未来扮演越来越重要的角色。

target_sen.target_sen: Significant advances have been made in the understanding of the biological and chemical nature of olfaction, but many fundamental questions have yet to be answered.
Researchers have still to decide whether smell is one sense or two - one responding to odours proper and the other registering odourless chemicals in the air.

syn_ary.synonym: inevitably set to play an increasingly important role

target_sen.target_sen: Questions like these mean that interest in the psychology of smell is inevitably set to play an increasingly important role for researchers.
Most of the research on smell undertaken to date has been of a physical scientific nature.
Questions like these mean that interest in the psychology of smell is inevitably set to play an increasingly important role for researchers.

questions.analysis: Section F主要讨论了气味在文化中的重要性，以及不同文化对气味的不同解释和价值观。这些内容与小标题v: "The interpretation of smells as a factor in defining groups"（气味的解释作为定义群体的一个因素）直接对应。文章中提到的“distinguish us from other cultures”和“Odours are invested with cultural values”是题目中“defining groups”和“interpretation of smells”的同义替换词。

central_sentences.sentence: Odours are invested with cultural values: smells that are considered to be offensive in some cultures may be perfectly acceptable in others. Therefore, our sense of smell is a means of, and model for, interacting with the world. Different smells can provide us with intimate and emotionally charged experiences and the value that we attach to these experiences is interiorised by the members of society in a deeply personal way. Importantly, our commonly held feelings about smells can help distinguish us from other cultures.
central_sentences.translation: 气味被赋予文化价值：在某些文化中被认为是令人不快的气味，在其他文化中可能完全可以接受。因此，我们的嗅觉是与世界互动的一种方式和模式。不同的气味可以给我们提供亲密和情感丰富的体验，我们赋予这些体验的价值被社会成员以一种深刻的个人方式内化。重要的是，我们对气味的共同感受可以帮助我们与其他文化区分开来。

target_sen.target_sen: Odours are invested with cultural values: smells that are considered to be offensive in some cultures may be perfectly acceptable in others.
Importantly, our commonly held feelings about smells can help distinguish us from other cultures.

questions.title: According to the introduction, we become aware of the importance of smell when

options.text: our ability to smell is damaged.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C. our ability to smell is damaged。文章中提到嗅觉的重要性被低估，并且人类的嗅觉能力相对较弱，这表明当我们的嗅觉能力受损时，我们才会意识到它的重要性。这与题干中的“become aware of the importance of smell”相对应。

central_sentences.sentence: While it is true that the olfactory powers of humans are nothing like as fine as those possessed by certain animals, they are still remarkably acute.
central_sentences.translation: 虽然人类的嗅觉能力确实不如某些动物那么敏锐，但它们仍然相当敏锐。

syn_ary.synonym: importance to our emotional and sensory lives

target_sen.target_sen: In spite of its importance to our emotional and sensory lives, smell is probably the most undervalued sense in many cultures.
In spite of its importance to our emotional and sensory lives, smell is probably the most undervalued sense in many cultures.

questions.title: The experiment described in paragraph B

options.text: shows how we make use of smell without realising it.
demonstrates that family members have a similar smell.
proves that a sense of smell is learnt.
compares the sense of smell in males and females.

questions.analysis: 文章描述的实验是关于人们在没有意识到的情况下如何通过气味识别出家人。这一点在 "even when not consciously considered, smells register" 中得到了明确的表达。题干中的正确选项A与这句描述直接对应，说明了我们在没有意识到的情况下使用气味。其他选项B、C和D的内容在文章中没有相应的支持。

central_sentences.sentence: Most of the subjects would probably never have given much thought to odour as a cue for identifying family members before being involved in the test, but as the experiment revealed, even when not consciously considered, smells register.
central_sentences.translation: 大多数受试者在参与这项测试之前可能从未考虑过气味作为识别家庭成员的线索，但正如实验所揭示的，即使在没有意识到的情况下，气味也会被记录下来。

target_sen.target_sen: Most of the subjects would probably never have given much thought to odour as a cue for identifying family members before being involved in the test, but as the experiment revealed, even when not consciously considered, smells register.

questions.title: What is the writer doing in paragraph C?
questions.analysis: 在这段话中，作者首先提到了一种普遍的看法，即人类的嗅觉能力不如某些动物。接着，作者反驳了这一看法，指出人类的嗅觉能力仍然非常敏锐。这种反驳的行为正是题干中所说的“rejecting a common belief”，因此正确答案是C。

central_sentences.sentence: While it is true that the olfactory powers of humans are nothing like as fine as those possessed by certain animals, they are still remarkably acute.
central_sentences.translation: 虽然人类的嗅觉能力确实不像某些动物那样敏锐，但它们仍然非常敏锐。

questions.title: What does the writer suggest about the study of smell in the atmosphere in paragraph E?

options.text: The measurement of smell is becoming more accurate.
Researchers believe smell is a purely physical reaction.
Smell is yet to be defined.

questions.analysis: 文章中提到“many fundamental questions have yet to be answered”和“Researchers have still to decide whether smell is one sense or two”，这些内容表明嗅觉的定义尚未确定。因此，正确答案是D：“Smell is yet to be defined”。

central_sentences.sentence: Significant advances have been made in the understanding of the biological and chemical nature of olfaction, but many fundamental questions have yet to be answered.
central_sentences.translation: 虽然在理解嗅觉的生物学和化学本质方面取得了重大进展，但许多基本问题仍有待解答。

central_sentences.sentence: Researchers have still to decide whether smell is one sense or two - one responding to odours proper and the other registering odourless chemicals in the air.
central_sentences.translation: 研究人员仍需决定嗅觉是一种感觉还是两种感觉——一种对正常气味作出反应，另一种感知空气中无味的化学物质。

target_sen.target_sen: Most of the research on smell undertaken to date has been of a physical scientific nature.
Most of the research on smell undertaken to date has been of a physical scientific nature.

groups.desc:
Questions 37-40

Complete the sentences.
Write ONE WORD ONLY from the text in each gap.
groups.collect:
Tests have shown that odours can help people recognise the$1633$belonging to their husbands and wives.
Certain linguistic groups may have difficulty describing smell because they lack the appropriate$1634$
The sense of smell may involve response to $1635$which do not smell, in addition to obvious odours.
Odours regarded as unpleasant in certain $1636$are not regarded as unpleasant in others.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“odours”在原文中对应“smell”，“recognise”对应“distinguish”，“husbands and wives”对应“marriage partners”。通过这些同义替换词，我们可以定位到原文中的答案句。答案句中明确提到，男女通过气味能够区分出他们的配偶穿过的“clothing”，因此正确答案是“clothing”。

central_sentences.sentence: In one well-known test, women and men were able to distinguish by smell alone clothing worn by their marriage partners from similar clothing worn by other people.
central_sentences.translation: 在一个著名的测试中，男性和女性仅凭气味就能够区分出他们的配偶穿过的衣服和其他人穿过的类似衣服。

target_sen.target_sen: One respondent to the survey believed that there is no true emotional bonding without touching and smelling a loved one.
In one well-known test, women and men were able to distinguish by smell alone clothing worn by their marriage partners from similar clothing worn by other people.

Most of the subjects would probably never have given much thought to odour as a cue for identifying family members before being involved in the test, but as the experiment revealed, even when not consciously considered, smells register.
In one well-known test, women and men were able to distinguish by smell alone clothing worn by their marriage partners from similar clothing worn by other people.
In one well-known test, women and men were able to distinguish by smell alone clothing worn by their marriage partners from similar clothing worn by other people.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“describing smell”对应原文中的“named”，而“lack the appropriate”对应“doesn't exist”。原文句子明确指出，许多语言中缺乏描述气味的特定词汇（vocabulary），因此答案是“vocabulary”。

central_sentences.sentence: Odours, unlike colours, for instance, cannot be named in many languages because the specific vocabulary simply doesn't exist.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，与颜色不同，在许多语言中无法命名气味，因为特定的词汇根本不存在。

target_sen.target_sen: Odours, unlike colours, for instance, cannot be named in many languages because the specific vocabulary simply doesn't exist.
Odours, unlike colours, for instance, cannot be named in many languages because the specific vocabulary simply doesn't exist.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "chemicals"。题干中的 "response to" 对应原文中的 "responding to"，"do not smell" 对应 "odourless"，"obvious odours" 对应 "odours proper"。通过这些对应关系，我们可以确定答案句中的 "chemicals" 是正确答案，因为它符合题干中描述的 "do not smell" 的特征。

central_sentences.sentence: Researchers have still to decide whether smell is one sense or two - one responding to odours proper and the other registering odourless chemicals in the air.
central_sentences.translation: 研究人员仍然需要决定嗅觉是一种感觉还是两种——一种对固有气味做出反应，另一种则感知空气中无气味的化学物质。

target_sen.target_sen: Most of the research on smell undertaken to date has been of a physical scientific nature.
Researchers have still to decide whether smell is one sense or two - one responding to odours proper and the other registering odourless chemicals in the air.
Other unanswered questions are whether the nose is the only part of the body affected by odours, and how smells can be measured objectively given the nonphysical components.
Questions like these mean that interest in the psychology of smell is inevitably set to play an increasingly important role for researchers.
Researchers have still to decide whether smell is one sense or two - one responding to odours proper and the other registering odourless chemicals in the air.
Researchers have still to decide whether smell is one sense or two - one responding to odours proper and the other registering odourless chemicals in the air.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是 "cultures"。题干中的 "unpleasant" 对应原文中的 "offensive"，"in certain" 对应 "in some"，"in others" 对应 "in others"。通过这些同义替换词的匹配，我们可以确定答案句中的 "cultures" 是填空的正确答案。题目考察的是不同文化对气味的不同接受度，这在原文中得到了明确的说明。

central_sentences.sentence: Odours are invested with cultural values: smells that are considered to be offensive in some cultures may be perfectly acceptable in others.
central_sentences.translation: 气味被赋予文化价值：在某些文化中被认为是令人不快的气味，在其他文化中可能完全可以接受。

target_sen.target_sen: Odours are invested with cultural values: smells that are considered to be offensive in some cultures may be perfectly acceptable in others.
Odours are invested with cultural values: smells that are considered to be offensive in some cultures may be perfectly acceptable in others.
Odours are invested with cultural values: smells that are considered to be offensive in some cultures may be perfectly acceptable in others.
